notes: Section 1201, , , , set forth Congressional findings and declaration of purpose relating to illegal flow of narcotics into United States.Pub. L. 98–473, title II, § 130298 Stat. 2168Oct. 12, 1984
Section 1202, , , , established National Drug Enforcement Policy Board.Pub. L. 98–473, title II, § 130398 Stat. 2168Oct. 12, 1984
Section 1203, , , , delineated responsibilities and functions of National Drug Enforcement Policy Board.Pub. L. 98–473, title II, § 130498 Stat. 2169Oct. 12, 1984
Section 1204, , , , related to reports to Congress.Pub. L. 98–473, title II, § 130598 Stat. 2170Oct. 12, 1984

Effective Date of Repeal, , , provided that the repeal of this chapter is effective on 30th day after first Director of National Drug Control Policy is confirmed by the Senate.Pub. L. 100–690, title I, § 1007(a)(3)102 Stat. 4187Nov. 18, 1988

Short Title, , , which provided that chapter XIII (§§ 1301 to 1307) of title II of was to be cited as the National Narcotics Act of 1984, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 98–473, title II, § 130198 Stat. 2168Pub. L. 98–473Pub. L. 100–690, title I, § 1007(a)(3)102 Stat. 4187Oct. 12, 1984Nov. 18, 1988

Executive Order No. 12590Ex. Ord. No. 12590, , 52 F.R. 10021, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 13284, § 11, , 68 F.R. 4076, provided for the establishment of a National Drug Policy Board, designated its membership and functions, and authorized coordinating groups.Mar. 26, 1987Jan. 23, 2003
